originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&pid_output` is never used.

presenceCondition: (and (= MARLIN_H true) (= PIDTEMP true) (= PID_DEBUG false))
model:
(define-fun PID_DEBUG () Bool
  false)
(define-fun MARLIN_H () Bool
  true)
(define-fun PIDTEMP () Bool
  true)
reasoning: When PIDTEMP=T AND PID_DEBUG=F: pid_output=1 at L11 is never read (no use of pid_output). Dead store. With PID_DEBUG=F, the #ifdef PID_DEBUG block (including SERIAL_ECHO_START) is skipped, so MARLIN_H=T does not cause a compile error. Compilable config. No '// ERROR' for pid_output dead store → new bug not in VBDB. The dead store occurs for any PIDTEMP=T compilable config; MARLIN_H=T is unnecessary. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
